title: hieracium verbascifolium subsp. thapsoides
intro: Hieracium verbascifolium subsp. thapsoides, also known as thapsi-leaved hawk's beard or Hieracium thapsoides, is a perennial plant species within the dandelion genus, characterized by its thapsi-shaped leaves and yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Hieracium verbascifolium subsp. thapsoides include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and act as an antiseptic agent, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in homeopathy to promote overall well-being and immune function.
